title: Lesson 51: When to use 'Para' or 'Por' (Part 2) + false friends
concepts: Here we'll carry on the difference between 'Para' and 'Por' and where to use them as well as a look at some words which aren't what they appear (false friends!)

sentences.english: I'm here for you (informal)
I'm here because of you (formal)
I'm here because of you (informal)
I have come because of you

sentences.spanish: Es por ti que hemos venido
sentences.english: It is because of you that we have come
I would change this one for that one (masculine things)
I would change these ones for those ones (masculine things)
I would change these ones for those ones (feminine things)
Would you change these ones for those ones (feminine things)?

sentences.spanish: ¿Por qué no cambiarías estas por esas?
sentences.english: Why wouldn't you change these ones for those ones (feminine things)?
Eight times eight
Thank you for calling
Thank you for calling me
I am tired of working (female)
I was tired of working (female)
I was tired of working (male/mixed group)
Its a way of doing it
My current job
Currently / at the moment

sentences.spanish: No creo en discutir de esta forma
sentences.english: I don't believe in arguing in this way
